title: Using the complementary event rule
problem: The probability that it will rain tomorrow is $\dfrac{3}{7}$. Find the probability that it will NOT rain tomorrow.

steps.working: The event is 'rain tomorrow'. Its complement is 'no rain tomorrow'. These two events are mutually exclusive and together cover all possible outcomes, so their probabilities must add to $1$.

title: Finding a complementary probability from a sample space
problem: A number is chosen at random from the integers $1$ to $20$ inclusive. Find the probability that the number chosen is NOT a multiple of $3$.

steps.working: Multiples of $3$ from $1$ to $20$: $3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18$ — that is $6$ numbers. $$P(\text{multiple of 3}) = \frac{6}{20} = \frac{3}{10}$$

title: At least one outcome — using the complement
problem: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. Find the probability that at least one die shows a $5$.

steps.working: The complement of 'at least one $5$' is 'no $5$ on either die'. It is much easier to calculate the complement first.
For each die, $P(\text{not a 5}) = \dfrac{5}{6}$. Since the dice are independent: $$P(\text{no 5 on either}) = \frac{5}{6} \times \frac{5}{6} = \frac{25}{36}$$
